steps.title: Use the given slope
steps.content:
A line with slope $-\frac{1}{3}$ can be written in slope-intercept form. Use $b$ for the y-intercept and start with this equation.
$$y=-\frac{x}{3}+b$$

steps.title: Find the intercept
steps.content:
Substitute the point $(9,10)$ into the equation to find $b$.
$$10=-\frac{9}{3}+b$$
$$10=-3+b$$
$$13=b$$

steps.title: Write the equation
steps.content: So the line is $y=-\frac{x}{3}+13$, which passes through $(9,10)$ and has the required slope. The answer is D.
